meta.purpose: Terminology boundaries, traps, and self-checks for Motion Design Master.
meta.load_condition: Load with Core for any generation, review, or refactor task in this domain.

standard_terms.definition: The repeatable rules of movement that make a product or video feel coherent.
The order in which the viewer's eye is guided through the scene.
The change of speed over time that gives motion weight, intent, and emotional tone.

banned_terms.why: It gives no intent, audience, hierarchy, or success criteria.
banned_terms.replace_with: Replace with specific criteria from the domain ontology.

banned_terms.why: Motion without hierarchy becomes noise and reduces perceived quality.
banned_terms.replace_with: Replace with specific criteria from the domain ontology.

banned_terms.why: Too vague unless translated into concrete constraints: restraint, rhythm, typography, spacing, and material behavior.
banned_terms.replace_with: Replace with specific criteria from the domain ontology.

misunderstandings.correct: World-class motion often removes movement to increase clarity, weight, and trust.
misunderstandings.key_distinction: World-class motion often removes movement to increase clarity, weight, and trust.
misunderstandings.why: Holding the wrong interpretation leads to poor judgment: World-class motion often removes movement to increase clarity, weight, and trust.
applies_when: Designing a dashboard data visualization where rapid value changes need to be read accurately
Animating a corporate logo reveal where brand stability and professionalism are prioritized
Creating micro-interactions for a primary call-to-action button that needs to feel solid and clickable
Designing transitions for a serious medical or financial application interface
does_not_apply_when: Creating a playful, high-energy mobile game tutorial that relies on constant feedback to keep users engaged
Designing a promotional splash screen for a music festival app where chaotic motion sets the mood
Building a particle physics simulation demo where showing every calculated element is the explicit goal
Animating a loading spinner that must remain in continuous motion to indicate active processing
misunderstandings.failure_risk: The agent will produce cluttered, distracting interfaces where unnecessary motion obscures critical data and erodes user trust.

misunderstandings.wrong: Animation quality starts with library choice.
misunderstandings.correct: Library choice matters after attention path, timing, intent, and information hierarchy are defined.
misunderstandings.key_distinction: Library choice matters after attention path, timing, intent, and information hierarchy are defined.
misunderstandings.why: Holding the wrong interpretation leads to poor judgment: Library choice matters after attention path, timing, intent, and information hierarchy are defined.
applies_when: Starting a new React component library design system project
Planning the onboarding flow sequence for a complex SaaS product
Architecting the state transitions for a multi-step checkout process
Defining the interaction model for a data-heavy analytics dashboard
does_not_apply_when: Prototyping a quick personal creative experiment where the goal is specifically to test a new web animation library
Implementing a straightforward hover effect on a button where timing and hierarchy are already standardized
Building a simple marketing landing page where the motion director has already provided pre-timed Lottie files
Debugging a performance issue that is strictly caused by a specific library's rendering pipeline
misunderstandings.failure_risk: The agent will lock the project into a technically convenient but narratively mismatched implementation, forcing awkward compromises in pacing and user guidance to fit the library's constraints.

self_check: Can a first-time viewer identify the main visual subject within one second?
Does every meaningful motion have a reason beyond decoration?
If 30% of the motion were removed, would the communication become clearer rather than weaker?
